entries.requirement: Source topology is parsed before validation, normalization, analysis, serialization, artifact emission, and CLI consumption.
entries.artifact_or_output: diagnostic JSON or parsed topology document
entries.evidence: CLI malformed JSON and successful compile tests exercise parse success and parse failure boundaries.

entries.requirement: Invalid JSON rejects input before AST construction with deterministic parser diagnostics.
entries.evidence: Malformed JSON fixture returns exit code 1 and PARSER.INVALID_JSON diagnostics.

entries.requirement: Parse, AST construction, and normalization are distinct logical phases; failed phases stop later phases.
entries.evidence: parse_topology is separate, but shape validation and normalization are co-located in validate_and_normalize/_validate_shape/_normalize.
entries.exact_gap: AST construction is not exposed as a standalone implementation boundary.

entries.requirement: Duplicate component, edge, and workload identifiers are rejected before IR construction.
entries.evidence: Duplicate identifier fixture returns normalization diagnostics and no successful artifact.

entries.requirement: Unresolved edge endpoints, workload roots, workload targets, and candidate components are rejected fail-closed.
entries.evidence: CLI tests cover unresolved edge endpoints, workload targets, and candidate components as fail-closed diagnostics with no successful artifact.

entries.requirement: Components, edges, workloads, roots, candidate sets, and adjacency entries use canonical lexicographic ordering.
entries.evidence: Schema/conformance tests include stable-ordering and equivalent AST/IR fixture coverage.

entries.requirement: Canonical JSON uses sorted keys, compact separators, UTF-8 bytes, and no trailing newline.
entries.artifact_or_output: canonical JSON text/bytes and sha256 hashes
entries.evidence: Canonical projection serialization test asserts stable compact sorted JSON output.

entries.requirement: Canonical IR is analysis-ready and representable without reachability, projection, or dependency evaluation.
entries.artifact_or_output: CanonicalIR typed object and IR schema payload
entries.evidence: Schema tests and architecture tests validate IR fixtures and immutable typed boundary use.

entries.requirement: Complement projection removes each candidate component and every incident edge while preserving unaffected graph structure.
entries.evidence: Projection implementation removes candidate nodes and outgoing references to candidates; schema fixtures cover projected object shapes.
entries.exact_gap: Implementation exposes only traversal-oriented ProjectedIR rather than full projected normalized IR contract shape.

entries.requirement: Reachability is directed path existence from any workload root to the workload target with deterministic traversal and cycle termination.
entries.evidence: Reachability profile conformance tests cover reachable, disconnected, multi-root, cycle, and self-loop fixtures.

entries.requirement: Dependency(S, W) is true exactly when Reach(W | not S) is empty.
entries.evidence: Predicate implementation applies projection then reachability; fixture/schema coverage exists for dependency result vectors.
entries.exact_gap: Semantic tests are not exhaustive across every dependency fixture.

entries.requirement: Structural classification is VALID, DEGRADED, or NULL and is not execution authorization or legitimacy.
entries.artifact_or_output: classification string in analysis, receipt, and artifact outputs
entries.evidence: Boundary tests enforce structural-only documentation; classification code emits only the three structural values.

entries.requirement: Compiler emits deterministic structural evidence artifacts and hash receipts that are not authority objects.
entries.evidence: CLI success and repeated execution tests verify artifact shape, no stdout/stderr, and byte-identical repeated output.

entries.requirement: CLI compile command exists, writes deterministic artifact JSON, emits machine-readable diagnostics, and uses stable exit codes.
entries.artifact_or_output: artifact file or diagnostic stderr JSON
entries.evidence: CLI integration tests cover success, malformed input, unresolved references, duplicates, missing input, emission failure, argument errors, and deterministic repeated execution.

entries.requirement: Every normative contract area is indexed and traceable to implementation, tests, artifacts, and status.
entries.evidence: Traceability validator checks spec refs, implementation/test paths, symbols, statuses, and missing correspondence failure behavior.

unspecified_public_behaviors.behavior: compile_artifact emits redundancy_map, k_of_n_resilience_profile, and annihilation_conditions fields.
unspecified_public_behaviors.evidence: Artifact schema lists these fields, but SPEC.md does not define their semantics beyond evidence artifact examples.

unspecified_public_behaviors.behavior: CLI accepts reserved flags --strict, --emit-diagnostics, and --emit-hash-receipt without changing output.
unspecified_public_behaviors.evidence: README documents stable command shape and exit codes but does not normatively define reserved flag behavior.
